CommandHistory.description:
Represents a command with optional raw string representation

This struct stores both the parsed command and optionally the original
string that was used to create it. This allows for preserving the exact
user input while still having access to the structured command data.

raw_string.description: The original string representation of the command, if available

oneOf.description: Inspect a single phase‑space point / momentum configuration

n_cores.description: Number of cores to parallelize over

samples.description: Number of random samples to evaluate

integrand_name.description: Integrand name inside the selected process

categories.description: Restrict the detailed view to the selected category tables

graphs.description: Restrict the detailed view to the selected master graph names

integrand_name.description: Integrand name inside the selected process

path.description: Optional dotted path to a specific setting
Optional dotted path to a specific setting
Optional dotted path to a specific setting

number_of_terms_in_epsilon_expansion.description:
The number of terms in the epsilon expansion to compute
Defaults is automatically inferred from input graph to get to the order O(epsilon^0)

numerical.description: Whether to evaluate numerically or not the resulting analytical expression

result_path.description: The path to store results in

Helicity.description: Helicity input. Use -1, 0, or 1 for fixed helicities; or use a string for summed modes.

n_points.description: Number of scaling points to sample

select.description: restrict test to particular graphs or limits

discrete_dim.description: The discrete dimensions of the sample

graph_id.description: Select a specific graph in momentum-space inspect

orientation_id.description: Select a specific orientation of the selected graph in momentum-space inspect

point.description: The point to inspect (x y) or (p0 px ...)

batch_size.description: Evaluate each iteration in per-core batches of this size

batch_timing.description: Target per-core batch wall time in seconds when batch size is not specified

integrand_name.description: The integrand name to integrate; repeat with -p to select multiple integrands

max_table_width.description: Maximum width used when normalizing integration status tables

min_time_between_status_updates.description: Minimum time between streamed in-iteration status updates

n_cores.description: Number of cores to parallelize over

no_show_integration_statistics.description: Hide integration statistics during intermediate iteration updates

no_stream_iterations.description: Disable streaming of end-of-iteration integration summaries in interactive terminals

no_stream_updates.description: Disable live streaming of in-iteration integration status updates

process.description: Process reference: #<id>, name:<name>, or <id>/<name>; repeat with -i to select multiple integrands

renderer.description: Renderer used for interactive streaming integration updates

restart.description: Whether to restart the integration from scratch, or continue from a previous run if possible

show_discrete_contributions_sum.description: Show the summed discrete contributions block independently of per-bin rows

show_max_weight_info.description: Show the overall max-weight details table

show_max_weight_info_for_discrete_bins.description: Show per-bin max-weight information for the first non-trivial discrete dimension

show_phase.description: Control which complex phase is shown in integration summary tables

show_summary_only.description: Only render the saved integration summary, without performing integration work

show_top_discrete_grid.description: Show monitoring rows for the first non-trivial discrete dimension

sort_contributions.description: Sort discrete contribution rows by index, absolute integral, or error

target.description: Specify a target as either `re im` / `re,im` for one shared target or `process@integrand=re,im`

uncorrelated.description: Use one independent sampling/training grid per selected integrand instead of a shared grid

workspace_path.description: The path to run the integrationg within

write_results_for_each_iteration.description: Keep per-iteration result and observable snapshot files in addition to the latest snapshots

title.description: Optional histogram title. Defaults to the observable name when omitted.

type_description.description: HwU TYPE description written after TYPE@ in the histogram header.

ParseableAtom.description: An atom that is serialized as a string. Do not make any assumptions about the state

oneOf.description: Load settings from TOML content passed as a CLI string
Set one or more dotted key-paths
Sync process runtime settings from the current default runtime settings

computation.description: Quantity computation mode: scalar projection per object, exact object count, or pairwise quantity.

order.description: Sorting direction applied together with ordering.

ordering.description: Ordering key used before entry_selection. Particle scalar quantities default to Quantity, jet scalar quantities default to PT, and pair quantities default to Quantity.

quantity.description: Per-object scalar quantity used when computation = "scalar".

clustered_pdgs.description: Optional list of PDG IDs allowed to be clustered. Use null to derive the default from the model, or a compact list like [-1,1,21,82] in CLI key-value assignments.

computation.description: Quantity computation mode: scalar projection per object, exact object count, or pairwise quantity.

order.description: Sorting direction applied together with ordering.

ordering.description: Ordering key used before entry_selection. Particle scalar quantities default to Quantity, jet scalar quantities default to PT, and pair quantities default to Quantity.

quantity.description: Per-object scalar quantity used when computation = "scalar".

integrand_name.description: Restrict the removal to a single integrand within the selected process

The name of the process to inspect
The amplitude name to renormalize

result_path.description: The directory to store per-graph results in

block_names.description: Command block names to execute (in order)

override_state.description: Save state to file after each call

path.description: Path to save the state to, by default is the current state folder

try_strings.description: Try to serialize using strings when saving run history

max.description: Optional upper bound. Use null to disable the upper cut.

min.description: Optional lower bound. Use null to disable the lower cut.

oneOf.description: Set the base directory for gammaloop state and logs

values.description: Any number of PARAM=COMPLEX pairs, or 'defaults' for process-targeted resets

oneOf.description: Load settings from TOML content passed as a CLI string
Set one or more dotted key-paths
Sync process runtime settings from the current default runtime settings

allow_symmetrization_of_external_fermion.description: Symmetrization (Option<bool> for smart defaults depending on generation type)

allowed_vertex_interactions.description: Restrict generation to an allow-list of vertex interactions

filter_selfenergies.description: Topology filters (Option<bool> to enable smart defaults)

loop_momentum_bases.description:
Graph processing toggles

Format:
  --loop-momentum-bases "GL_12=7,10 GL_77=4,2"
  --select-graphs "GL_12 GL_13"
  --veto-graphs "GL_11 GL_15"

n_cut_blobs.description:
Cut options (cross-section)
Range of cut blobs on either side of the cut

n_cut_spectators.description: Range of cut spectators on either side of the cut

number_of_fermion_loops.description: Number of closed fermion loops; negative disables

tokens.description:
Quote-free spec.

Grammar (unquoted): `INITIAL to FINAL [process_options] [generation_options]`
Also accepts: `INITIAL > FINAL [ ... ]`

Examples:
  e+ e- to d d~ g veto u d xs QED^2==2 QCD^2<=4 pert loops=1 fsloops=2 QCD=2
  e+ e- > { z z, a a } [ {1} {{2}} QCD=2 QED=1 ] / u d | g ghG QED==2 QCD>=2 QCD<=4

Notes:
- `veto` and `only` are synonyms for `/` and `|`.
- The `[ ... ]` block holds perturbative spec: `{n}`, `{{n}}`, and orders `QCD=n` with shorthand `QCD`≡1.

n_points.description: Number of scaling points to sample
